narrative_angle: Mixed audience strategy: open with undeniable gameplay fantasy, then bridge directly into traction and execution proof. Balance publisher confidence (marketability + launch readiness) with investor confidence (scalable upside + milestone discipline). Current strength center: traction, team.

product.reasoning: Gameplay clarity and unique hook are well evidenced.

traction.reasoning: Based on quantity and concreteness of KPIs / external validation signals.

business_model.reasoning: Monetization and go-to-market are clear enough for a first pass.

team.reasoning: Execution credibility inferred from team completeness and leadership coverage.

key_selling_points: Gameplay-first pitch opening to win attention in under 30 seconds.
Evidence-led traction narrative: only hard signals, no buzzword inflation.
Competitive differentiation framed through player fantasy + execution edge.
Clear milestone ask that maps partner capital to measurable outcomes.
Team credibility supports delivery risk mitigation.
Development stage clarity: Vertical slice completed.
risks: Overloading slides with detail despite strong fundamentals.
how_to_position_them: Keep deck to high-signal proof points and use backup slides for depth.

recommended_slides.title: Hook + Gameplay Clarity
recommended_slides.objective: Show the core loop and player fantasy immediately.

recommended_slides.title: Traction Signals
recommended_slides.objective: Present KPIs, wishlists, tests, and external validation.

recommended_slides.title: Differentiation
recommended_slides.objective: Explain why this game wins versus direct alternatives.

recommended_slides.title: Execution Credibility
recommended_slides.objective: Prove team capability, roadmap realism, and delivery plan.

recommended_slides.title: Business Model + Market
recommended_slides.objective: Show monetization logic and market timing for upside.

recommended_slides.title: The Ask + Next Milestones
recommended_slides.objective: Make the partnership decision clear and time-bound.
